briefdescription.para: Status Register.
Interrupt Status Register.
Interrupt Enable Register.
Reconfig Register.
Shift bits for Edge.
Shift bits for Edge.
Prediv2 3.
Prediv2 3.
MX.
Prediv2 3.
FBout enable.
MX 3.
Mask for Charge Pump.
Shift bits for Res.
Shift bits for Res.
Shift bits for FB DELAY.
Shift bits for Reference DELAY.
Shift bits for DIVCLK_EDGE.
Shift bits for DIVCLK_EDGE.
Mask for DIVCLK_EDGE.
Shift bits for Prediv2.
Shift bits for MUX.
Shift bits for P5EN.
Shift bits for P5EDGE.
Mask for P5EDGE.

sectiondef.header: Bitmasks and offsets of XCLK_WIZ_ISR_OFFSET register

description.para: This register is used to display interrupt status register

briefdescription.para: User clock 3 stopped.
User clock 2 stopped.
User clock 1 stopped.
User clock 0 stopped.
User clock 3 has glitch.
User clock 2 has glitch.
User clock 1 has glitch.
User clock 0 has glitch.
User clock 3 is less than specification.
User clock 2 is less than specification.
User clock 1 is less than specification.
User clock 0 is less than specification.
User clock 3 is max than specification.
User clock 2 is max than specification.
User clock 1 is max than specification.
User clock 0 is max than specification.
User clock[0-3] has stopped.
User clock[0-3] has glitch.
User clock[0-3] is min than specification.
User clock[0-3] is max than specification.
Shift bits for User clock 3 stop.
Shift bits for User clock 2 stop.
Shift bits for User clock 1 stop.
Shift bits for User clock 0 stop.
Shift bits for User clock 3 glitch.
Shift bits for User clock 2 glitch.
Shift bits for User clock 1 glitch.
Shift bits for User clock 0 glitch.
Shift bits for User clock 3 less.
Shift bits for User clock 2 less.
Shift bits for User clock 1 less.
Shift bits for User clock 0 less.
Shift bits for User clock 3 max.
Shift bits for User clock 2 max.
Shift bits for User clock 1 max.
Shift bits for User clock 0 max.

sectiondef.header: Bitmasks and offsets of XCLK_WIZ_IER_OFFSET register

description.para: This register is used to display interrupt status register

briefdescription.para: User clock 3 stopped.
User clock 2 stopped.
User clock 1 stopped.
User clock 0 stopped.
User clock 3 has glitch.
User clock 2 has glitch.
User clock 1 has glitch.
User clock 0 has glitch.
User clock 3 is less than specification.
User clock 2 is less than specification.
User clock 1 is less than specification.
User clock 0 is less than specification.
User clock 3 is max than specification.
User clock 2 is max than specification.
User clock 1 is max than specification.
User clock 0 is max than specification.
Shift bits for User clock 3 stop.
Shift bits for User clock 2 stop.
Shift bits for User clock 1 stop.
Shift bits for User clock 0 stop.
Shift bits for User clock 3 glitch.
Shift bits for User clock 2 glitch.
Shift bits for User clock 1 glitch.
Shift bits for User clock 0 glitch.
Shift bits for User clock 3 less.
Shift bits for User clock 2 less.
Shift bits for User clock 1 less.
Shift bits for User clock 0 less.
Shift bits for User clock 3 max.
Shift bits for User clock 2 max.
Shift bits for User clock 1 max.
Shift bits for User clock 0 max.
Callback type for all interrupts defined.

parameterdescription.para: is a callback reference passed in by the upper layer when setting the callback functions, and passed back to the upper layer when the callback is invoked.
is a bit mask indicating the cause of the event. For current core version, this parameter is "OR" of 0 or more XCLK_WIZ_ISR_*_MASK constants defined in xclmon_hw.h.

briefdescription.para: This routine is a stub for the asynchronous error interrupt callback.

detaileddescription.para: The stub is here in case the upper layer forgot to set the handler. On initialization, Error interrupt handler is set to this callback. It is considered an error for this handler to be invoked.

parameterdescription.para: is a callback reference passed in by the upper layer when setting the callback functions, and passed back to the upper layer when the callback is invoked.
is a bit mask indicating the cause of the error. Its value equals 'OR'ing one or more XCLK_WIZ_ISR_*_MASK values defined in xclk_wiz_hw.h.

briefdescription.para: Initialize the XClk_Wiz instance provided by the caller based on the given Config structure.

parameterdescription.para: is the XClk_Wiz instance to operate on.
is the device configuration structure containing information about a specific CLK_WIZ.
is the base address of the device. If address translation is being used, then this parameter must reflect the virtual base address. Otherwise, the physical address should be used.

briefdescription.para: Calculate the M, D, and O values for the given SetRate frequency.

parameterdescription.para: is the XClk_Wiz instance to operate on.
is the frequency for which the M, D and O values are to be calculated.

briefdescription.para: Calculate the M, D, and O values for the given SetRate frequency.

parameterdescription.para: is the XClk_Wiz instance to operate on.
is the frequency for which the M, D and O values are to be calculated in Hz.

briefdescription.para: Set the Minimum error that can be tolerated.

parameterdescription.para: is the XClk_Wiz instance to operate on.
is the error margin that can be tolerated in Hz.

simplesect.para: Should be called only if there is only one output clock.

briefdescription.para: Change the frequency to the given rate in Hz.

parameterdescription.para: is the XClk_Wiz instance to operate on.
is the frequency in Hz to be set.

simplesect.para: Should be called only if there is only one output clock.

briefdescription.para: Get the clock frequency for the given ClockId.

parameterdescription.para: is the XClk_Wiz instance to operate on.
is the output clock.
clock rate in Hz.

listitem.para: XST_SUCCESS getting the frequency was successful.

briefdescription.para: Set the clock rate frequency for the given ClockId.

parameterdescription.para: is the XClk_Wiz instance to operate on.
is the output clock.
clock rate in Hz.

listitem.para: XST_SUCCESS Setting the rate was successful.

briefdescription.para: Change the frequency to the given rate.

parameterdescription.para: is the XClk_Wiz instance to operate on.
is the frequency for which is requested in MHz.

simplesect.para: Should be called only if there is only one output clock.

briefdescription.para: Enable Clock for the given ClockId.

parameterdescription.para: is the XClk_Wiz instance to operate on.
is the output clock.

briefdescription.para: Disable Clock for the given ClockId.

parameterdescription.para: is the XClk_Wiz instance to operate on.
is the output clock.

briefdescription.para: Wait till the clocking wizard is locked to the frequency.

parameterdescription.para: is the XClk_Wiz instance to operate on.

briefdescription.para: Change the Input frequency to the given rate.

parameterdescription.para: is the XClk_Wiz instance to operate on.
is the frequency for which is to be set.

simplesect.para: Should be called only if the input provider clock is changed eg input clock is si570.

briefdescription.para: Get the Input frequency.

parameterdescription.para: is the XClk_Wiz instance to operate on.

simplesect.para: Should be called only if the input provider clock is changed eg input clock is si570.

briefdescription.para: XClk_Wiz_GetInterruptSettings will get the information from clock wizard IER and ISR Registers.

parameterdescription.para: is the XClk_Wiz instance to operate on.

briefdescription.para: XCLK_WIZ_BIT_SET is used to set bit in a register.

parameterdescription.para: is a base address of IP
is offset where the register is present
is the part of the bitname before _MASK

briefdescription.para: XCLK_WIZ_BIT_RESET is used to reset bit in a register.

parameterdescription.para: is a base address of IP
is offset where the register is present
is the part of the bitname before _MASK

memberdef.argsstring: (UINTPTR BaseAddress, u32 RegisterOffset, u32 BitMask, u32 BitShift)
memberdef.name: XCLK_WIZ_GET_BITFIELD_VALUE

briefdescription.para: XCLK_WIZ_GET_BITFIELD_VALUE is used to get the value of bitfield from register.

parameterdescription.para: is a base address of IP
is offset where the register is present
is the part of the bitname before _MASK or _SHIFT

simplesect.para: Bit Field Value in u32 format

memberdef.argsstring: (UINTPTR BaseAddress, u32 RegisterOffset, u32 BitMask, u32 BitShift, u32 Value)
memberdef.name: XCLK_WIZ_SET_BITFIELD_VALUE

briefdescription.para: XCLK_WIZ_SET_BITFIELD_VALUE is used to set the value of bitfield from register.

parameterdescription.para: is a base address of IP
is offset where the register is present
is the part of the bitname before _MASK or _SHIFT
is to be set. Passed in u32 format.

briefdescription.para: XClk_Wiz_IntrEnable is used to set interrupt mask to enable interrupts.

detaileddescription.para: This is done before enabling the core.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the CLK_WIZ Instance to be worked on.
Interrupts to be enabled.

briefdescription.para: XClk_Wiz_GetIntrEnable is used to find out which interrupts are registered for system.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the CLK_WIZ Instance to be worked on.

briefdescription.para: XClk_Wiz_IntrDisable is used to disable interrupts.

detaileddescription.para: This is after disabling the core.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the CLK_WIZ Instance to be worked on.
Interrupts to be disabled.

briefdescription.para: XClk_Wiz_IntrGetIrq is used to find out which events have triggered the interrupt.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the CLK_WIZ Instance to be worked on.

briefdescription.para: XClk_Wiz_IntrAckIrq is acknowledge the events.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the CLK_WIZ Instance to be worked on.
is Bit Mask for ack of interrupts.

briefdescription.para: Looks up the device configuration based on the unique device ID/BaseAddress.

detaileddescription.para: The XClk_Wiz_ConfigTable[] contains the configuration info for each device in the system.

parameterdescription.para: contains the base address of the device

simplesect.para: A pointer to the configuration found or NULL if the specified device ID/BaseAddress was not found. See xclk_wiz.h for the definition of XClk_Wiz_Config.
In XSCT/classic flow, DeviceId is used to look up the device configuration.

briefdescription.para: This routine installs an asynchronous callback function for the given HandlerType:

para.preformatted: HandlerType Invoked by this driver when: ----------------------- -------------------------------------------------- XCLK_WIZ_HANDLER_CLK_OUTOF_RANGE Clock under flow/over flow XCLK_WIZ_HANDLER_CLK_GLITCH Clock Glitch XCLK_WIZ_HANDLER_CLK_STOP Clock Stop XCLK_WIZ_HANDLER_OTHERERROR Any other type of interrupts

parameterdescription.para: is the XClk_Wiz instance to operate on
is the type of call back to be registered.
is the pointer to a call back function which is called when a particular event occurs.
is a void pointer to data to be referenced to by the CallBackFunc

simplesect.para: Invoking this function for a handler that already has been installed replaces it with the new handler.

briefdescription.para: Macro to read register.

parameterdescription.para: is the base address of CLK_WIZ
is the register offset.

simplesect.para: C-style signature: u32 XClk_Wiz_ReadReg(UINTPTR BaseAddress, u32 RegOffset)

briefdescription.para: Macro to write to register.

parameterdescription.para: is the base address of CLK_WIZ
is the register offset.
is the value to be written to the register.

simplesect.para: C-style signature: void XClk_Wiz_WriteReg(UINTPTR BaseAddress, u32 RegOffset, u32 Data)

briefdescription.para: XClk_Wiz_InterruptEnable will enable the interrupts present in the interrupt mask passed onto the function.

parameterdescription.para: is the XClk_Wiz instance to operate on
is the interrupt mask which need to be enabled in core

briefdescription.para: XClk_Wiz_InterruptDisable will disable the interrupts present in the interrupt mask passed onto the function.

parameterdescription.para: is the XClk_Wiz instance to operate on
is the interrupt mask which need to be enabled in core

briefdescription.para: XClk_Wiz_InterruptGetEnabled will get the interrupt mask set (enabled) in the CLK_WIZ core.

parameterdescription.para: is the XClk_Wiz instance to operate on

simplesect.para: Interrupt Mask with bits set for corresponding interrupt in Interrupt enable register

briefdescription.para: XClk_Wiz_InterruptGetStatus will get the list of interrupts pending in the Interrupt Status Register of the CLK_WIZ core.

parameterdescription.para: is the XClk_Wiz instance to operate on

simplesect.para: Interrupt Mask with bits set for corresponding interrupt in Interrupt Status register

briefdescription.para: XClk_Wiz_InterruptClear will clear the interrupts set in the Interrupt Status Register of the CLK_WIZ core.

parameterdescription.para: is the XClk_Wiz instance to operate on
is Interrupt Mask with bits set for corresponding interrupt to be cleared in the Interrupt Status register

briefdescription.para: This function is the interrupt handler for the CLK_WIZ core.

detaileddescription.para: This handler reads the pending interrupt from the Interrupt Status register determines the source of the interrupts and calls the respective callbacks for the interrupts that are enabled in Interrupt Enable register and finally clears the interrupts.

The application is responsible for connecting this function to the interrupt system. Application beyond this core is also responsible for providing callbacks to handle interrupts and installing the callbacks using XClk_Wiz_SetCallBack() during initialization phase.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XClk_Wiz core instance.

simplesect.para: Interrupt should be enabled to execute interrupt handler.

briefdescription.para: All interrupts enable mask.
All interrupts enable shift bits.
All interrupt status register mask.
All interrupts status register shift.
This file implements the functions to get the CLK_WIZ GUI information and Clock Monitor Interrupt status.

detaileddescription.para: Look up the hardware settings using device ID.
This file implements the functions which handle the interrupts in the CLK_WIZ Controller.

para.preformatted:
Ver Who Date Changes
1.0 ram 2/12/16 Initial version for Clock Wizard 1.1 siv 8/17/16 Used UINTPTR instead of u32 for Baseaddress Changed the prototype of XClk_Wiz_CfgInitialize 1.2 ms 3/02/17 Fixed compilation warnings. Fix for CR-970507. 1.3 sd 4/09/20 Added versal support. 1.4 sd 5/22/20 Added zynqmp set rate. Use PrimInClkFreq for input clock rate. sd 8/12/20 Added a setrate function that takes the rate in Hz. 1.5 sd 5/22/20 Prevent return in void function 1.6 sd 7/07/23 Added SDT support. 1.8 sd 8/14/24 Added GetRate support. 1.11 sd 9/27/25 Added XClk_Wiz_GetInputRate. sd 10/29/25 Conditionally compile XClk_Wiz_GetInputRate. 1.12 sd 11/21/25 Fix a typo in strcmp. 1.13 sd 5/7/26 Add the CP and Res update on rate change.

Ver Who Date Changes
1.0 ram 02/12/16 Initial version for Clock Wizard 1.3 sd 4/09/20 Added versal support. 1.4 sd 5/22/20 Added zynqmp set rate. Add Enable and Disable clock APIs.

Ver Who Date Changes
1.0 ram 02/12/16 Initial version for Clock Wizard 1.2 ms 03/02/17 Fixed compilation errors. Fix for CR-970507. 1.6 sd 07/07/23 Added SDT support. 1.11 vlt 01/27/26 Fixed codespell issues

detaileddescription.para: The hardware setting is inside the configuration table in xclk_wiz_g.c, generated automatically by XPS or manually by the user.

para.preformatted:
Ver Who Date Changes
1.0 ram 02/12/16 Initial version for Clock Wizard 1.6 sd 7/07/23 Added SDT support. 1.11 vlt 12/12/25 Update Doxygen comments to include SDT flow details.
